checklist.notes: Properly implements gspread client initialization with service account credentials from GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS. Uses required scopes for Sheets and Drive APIs. Includes CredentialsError exception for missing/invalid credentials. Validated with 3 unit tests covering success, missing credentials, and file not found scenarios.

Integrates with GOOGLE_DRIVE_ROOT_FOLDER_ID from settings. Copy operation correctly uses folder_id parameter to place sheets in Shared Drive. Validates folder ID format at settings level. Tested in TestSheetCreationDetails::test_create_project_sheet_sets_correct_folder.

Implements async create_project_sheet() method with proper error handling. Uses gspread.copy() to duplicate template sheets. Returns SheetResult dataclass with sheet_id, url, title, template_type, and created_at. Handles TemplateNotFoundError when template missing. 6 unit tests cover success, errors, and all template types.

Supports all 6 template types (aggregators, opr, mpp, adop, adre, commercial) via TemplateType enum. Uses settings.get_template_sheet_id() to map template names to sheet IDs. Template IDs validated at settings level (min 20 chars, no placeholders). Includes validate_templates() method to check all 6 templates at startup. 3 tests validate template accessibility checking.

Implements async share_sheet() method with role validation (reader, writer, owner). Uses gspread spreadsheet.share() with proper perm_type='user'. Handles SpreadsheetNotFound and permission errors with SheetOperationError. 4 unit tests cover success, invalid role, sheet not found, and permission errors.

Implements read-back validation via read_back_validate() method. Uses worksheet.acell() to read individual cells by reference. Handles None cell values by converting to empty string. Returns ValidationResult with matches, mismatches, and per-field details. 6 unit tests cover match/mismatch scenarios, whitespace stripping, and error handling.

Implements async populate_sheet() method with batch_update() for efficiency. Maps 17 content fields to cells B2-B18 via COMMON_FIELD_MAPPING. Skips None and empty values. Converts non-string values to strings. Returns PopulateResult with write counts and failure details. 9 unit tests cover success, empty values, field mapping, and edge cases.

Uses worksheet.batch_update() for single API call efficiency (reduces 17 fields from 17 calls to 1 call, 94% reduction). Builds list of {range, values} dicts before batch operation. Only calls batch_update() if updates list is non-empty. Tested in TestPopulationDetails with multiple scenarios including empty content dict.

Implements exponential backoff retry logic with MAX_RETRIES=3, INITIAL_RETRY_DELAY=1.0s, MAX_RETRY_DELAY=16.0s. _retry_operation() wrapper handles Google Sheets 429 errors specifically. Exponential backoff calculation: min(base_delay * 2^attempt, MAX_RETRY_DELAY). Uses time.sleep() between retries. 6 unit tests validate backoff calculation, retry on 429, max retries exhausted, and transient error handling.

Custom exception hierarchy: SheetsManagerError (base) with 4 subclasses (CredentialsError, TemplateNotFoundError, SheetOperationError, RateLimitError). All public methods have try/except blocks with proper exception chaining. Detailed error messages with context. Structured logging at info/warning/error levels. 4 exception tests validate hierarchy and raising. Multiple error scenario tests throughout suite.

missing_lines: 251-265: Non-429 API error handling path in retry logic
280: RateLimitError raise after max retries (tested but not coverage-detected)
467-468: Field value conversion error handling (edge case)

code_quality.error_messages: PASS - Detailed error messages with context
code_quality.logging: PASS - Structured logging with appropriate levels
code_quality.async_implementation: PASS - Async wrapper over sync gspread using asyncio.to_thread()

integration_validation.settings_integration: PASS - Uses get_settings() for all configuration
integration_validation.enum_integration: PASS - Uses TemplateType enum for type safety
integration_validation.job_manager_integration: PASS - Imported and used in job_manager.py _step_populate_sheet() method
integration_validation.field_mapping: PASS - 17 fields mapped (4 SEO + 7 content + 6 project data)
integration_validation.template_support: PASS - All 6 template types supported (aggregators, opr, mpp, adop, adre, commercial)

performance.batch_operations: PASS - Uses batch_update() for 94% API call reduction
performance.async_pattern: PASS - Non-blocking async wrapper over sync gspread client
performance.memory_efficiency: PASS - Dataclasses instead of heavy objects, no caching
performance.rate_limiting: PASS - Exponential backoff prevents API quota exhaustion

recommendations.item: Consider running integration test with real credentials in CI/CD to validate end-to-end flow
recommendations.rationale: Current integration test is skipped. Real API validation would catch permission or template access issues early.

recommendations.item: Add multi-language support for AR and RU columns (C and D)
recommendations.rationale: COMMON_FIELD_MAPPING currently only maps to column B (EN). Templates have AR (C) and RU (D) columns but no API support yet.

recommendations.item: Consider template caching to avoid repeated open_by_key() calls
recommendations.rationale: Template validation and creation both call open_by_key(). Caching could reduce API calls further, but current implementation is already efficient.

recommendations.item: Add metrics collection for retry counts and API latency
recommendations.rationale: Would help identify rate limit patterns and optimize backoff strategy. Not critical for MVP.

strengths: Comprehensive test coverage at 90% with 56 test cases covering all major paths
Robust error handling with custom exception hierarchy and proper chaining
Efficient batch update pattern reduces API calls by 94%
Exponential backoff retry logic handles rate limits gracefully
Full type hints and Pydantic validation at settings level
Clean async implementation that doesn't block event loop
Excellent documentation with usage guide and implementation summary
Follows existing codebase patterns (similar to AuthService, ProjectService)
Template validation method allows startup health checks
Dataclass-based results provide structured, type-safe responses

environment_variables: GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS - Path to service account JSON key file
GOOGLE_DRIVE_ROOT_FOLDER_ID - Shared Drive folder ID for sheet storage
TEMPLATE_SHEET_ID_AGGREGATORS - Template sheet ID for aggregators
TEMPLATE_SHEET_ID_OPR - Template sheet ID for OPR
TEMPLATE_SHEET_ID_MPP - Template sheet ID for MPP
TEMPLATE_SHEET_ID_ADOP - Template sheet ID for ad operations
TEMPLATE_SHEET_ID_ADRE - Template sheet ID for ad revenue
TEMPLATE_SHEET_ID_COMMERCIAL - Template sheet ID for commercial
service_account_permissions: Google Sheets API enabled on GCP project
Google Drive API enabled on GCP project
Service account has Editor access to Shared Drive
Service account can read all 6 template sheets
Service account can create files in root folder

field_mapping_validation.mapping_validated: PASS - All fields tested in TestFieldMappingDetails
field_mapping_validation.cell_format_validated: PASS - Regex pattern ^[A-Z]+\d+$ validated for all cells

overall_assessment: EXCELLENT - SheetsManager implementation is production-ready with comprehensive test coverage (90%), robust error handling, efficient API usage, and proper integration with the existing codebase. All 10 QA checklist items passed validation. The module follows Python best practices with full type hints, async/await patterns, and clean architecture. Minor recommendations are for future enhancements only, not blocking issues. Ready for Phase 5 integration.

validation_summary.error_handling: Robust with custom exceptions and retries
validation_summary.performance: Optimized with batch updates and async patterns
validation_summary.code_quality: High quality with type hints and PEP 8 compliance
